title: The One With The Screamer

jokes.text: That's the word l use when l can't remember the real thing.
jokes.setup: Monica explains where her dad is at 'Flimbees'
jokes.punchline: Reveals she made up the word because she can't remember the actual place
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Monica's quirks, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My dad is at Flimbees.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I have no idea what Flimbees is. I can't remember the name of the place, so I just made up a word.

jokes.text: lt's Chandler and us versus Monica and them.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to hang up the phone, others don't
jokes.punchline: Ross creates battle lines between friends over a phone call
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the conflict, shows Ross getting worked up over minor things

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Guys, I need to hang up the phone.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, wait! I'm not done talking to Janice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I wanted to tell Rachel about my new song.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, Mon, just five more minutes!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know what? This is ridiculous. Either we hang up the phone now, or we don't. There's no middle ground here.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm talking about drawing a line in the sand. You're either with Monica on this, or you're against her. And if you're against her, you're against all of us.

jokes.text: Whose Number Two? One of the more difficult games sewer workers play.
jokes.setup: Someone asks 'Who's number two?' about tickets
jokes.punchline: Chandler mishears as 'Whose Number Two' and creates absurd sewer worker game
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler wordplay with absurd twist, perfect character integration and economy

dialogue.speaker: Character
dialogue.line: Who's number two?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whose Number Two? That's a great sewer worker game! You get a plunger, you get a badge, and you get to say 'I'm going down!' a lot.

jokes.text: Bottle next to the shaving cream. -Worm medicine for the duck.
jokes.setup: Joey says he borrowed Chandler's cologne but Chandler says he doesn't have cologne
jokes.punchline: Reveals Joey used duck worm medicine thinking it was cologne
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd reveal that fits Joey's character, great setup/payoff structure

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, can I borrow some of your cologne?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't have cologne.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? But I've been using your stuff for weeks!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's duck worm medicine.

jokes.text: Monica, l'm scared! -Honey, that's a sleeve.
jokes.setup: Chandler is trying to get dressed while Monica stays on hold
jokes.punchline: Chandler gets confused/tangled in clothing, Monica has to help
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment, competent but not special

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm on hold with the phone company. This could take a while.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No problem, I'll just get dressed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why is my arm stuck in the pant leg?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because you're putting them on backwards, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that would explain the chafing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Here, let me help you.

jokes.text: Can you take a duck and a chick to the theater? -Of course not. -l just wanted them to hear it from somebody else.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if pets can come to theater, Monica says no
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals he knew the answer but wanted the pets to hear rejection from someone else
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Joey's relationship with his pets, good structure

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Can pets come to the theater?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, Joey, pets can't come to the theater.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know. I just wanted them to hear it from someone else.

jokes.text: First ones here!
jokes.setup: They arrive at theater and note they're first
jokes.punchline: Tommy repeats it enthusiastically, showing his aggressive personality
jokes.explanation: Minor character establishment moment, not particularly funny on its own

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: We're first! We're first!

jokes.text: Oh, Tommy, this is Ross. Ross, Tommy. -Oh, l'm sorry. This is Cailin. Cailin-- -Sorry. -And break!
jokes.setup: Awkward introductions between Rachel's new date and her ex Ross
jokes.punchline: The painful small talk ends abruptly with 'And break!' like a rehearsal
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with theatrical callback that fits the setting

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Um, Tommy, this is Ross. Ross, Tommy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, great to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: Yeah, you too, man.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So... nice weather we're having.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, really great. Very... weathery.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, this is going great.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And break!

jokes.text: Well, this is... -...awkward. -Well, because Rachel and l used to go out. -Oh, l didn't know that. -Well, then this is awkward.
jokes.setup: Ross notes the situation is awkward, explains why
jokes.punchline: Tommy's deadpan repetition of 'this is awkward' after learning the info
jokes.explanation: Nice repetition for emphasis, captures the uncomfortable moment well

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: This is awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: This is awkward.

jokes.text: You thought? Well, that didn't really work out for you. ldiot! What are you, a moron? lt says, 'D-1 3'! Okay? You're surrounded by even numbers! Does that give you a clue?
jokes.setup: Someone is in the wrong theater seat
jokes.punchline: Tommy's explosive, disproportionate rage over a simple seating mistake
jokes.explanation: Establishes Tommy as the 'screamer' character effectively, good escalation

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: That's my seat!

dialogue.speaker: Other Person
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry, I didn't realize—

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: Didn't realize?! DIDN'T REALIZE?! I've been coming to this theater for SEVEN YEARS! SEVEN YEARS! And you just waltz in here like you own the place?!

dialogue.speaker: Other Person
dialogue.line: It's just a seat, there are plenty of—

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: JUST A SEAT?! You know what? That's it! I'm calling the manager! I'm calling the police! I'm calling the news! Everyone needs to know about this TRAVESTY!

jokes.text: Almost as good as the play with the two naked girls on the seesaw. -l wasn't in that. -l know.
jokes.setup: Ross compliments Joey's performance by comparing to another play
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifies he wasn't in that play, Ross deadpans 'I know'
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure with economical delivery, solid timing

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That was amazing! You were almost as good as when you played Mercutio in Romeo and Juliet!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ross, I was never in Romeo and Juliet.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: l ran into you in the hallway in your building. lt was right after l slept with Joey. He dumped me the next day.
jokes.setup: Lauren recognizes Monica, explains how they know each other
jokes.punchline: Overshares intimate details about Joey dumping her after sex
jokes.explanation: Typical sitcom awkward oversharing moment, serviceable but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Lauren
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica! Hi! We went to high school together!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh wow, Lauren! How have you been?

dialogue.speaker: Lauren
dialogue.line: Good, good. Yeah, I've been great. Actually, you know who I ran into? Joey Tribbiani!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh really? That's nice.

dialogue.speaker: Lauren
dialogue.line: Yeah, we went out a few times. And let me tell you, Monica, the things that guy can do— I mean, we're talking hours. Hours! And then after, he just got up and left. Didn't even say goodbye. Just walked out the door like I wasn't even there.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wow, that's... I'm sorry that happened.

dialogue.speaker: Lauren
dialogue.line: I mean, the stamina on that man is incredible, but his manners? Terrible.

jokes.text: The short version is, l pursued her for a couple of months. Then l gave her a check for $20,000 and she was mine.
jokes.setup: Someone asks how Pete and Monica met
jokes.punchline: Pete casually admits to essentially buying Monica's affection
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, good character work for wealthy Pete

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How did you and Pete meet?

dialogue.speaker: Pete
dialogue.line: Well, I saw Monica at this restaurant, and I just knew I had to have her. So I bought her a drink, then dinner, then a apartment...

jokes.text: ln the long version, l dump him for telling people the short version.
jokes.setup: Pete's admission about paying for Monica
jokes.punchline: Monica threatens to dump him for admitting it publicly
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with snappy delivery, shows Monica's embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You just told everyone you paid for me! That's so embarrassing!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm breaking up with you!

jokes.text: With her face and her chest, l could really put something together. -Could l borrow it?
jokes.setup: Estelle comments on Monica's physical attributes for acting
jokes.punchline: Monica asks if she can borrow 'it' - unclear if face, chest, or talent
jokes.explanation: Clever ambiguous pronoun reference, good timing

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: You have such a pretty face, and a great chest, and real talent.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can I borrow it?

jokes.text: Tommy's in line for the bathroom and someone just cut. l think he's gonna snap.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains where Tommy is
jokes.punchline: Predicts Tommy will have another rage episode over bathroom line
jokes.explanation: Builds on established character trait, good setup for more Tommy drama

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Tommy's in the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He'll probably have another rage episode over the bathroom line.

jokes.text: l don't know. Could be because l don't feel like waiting all night for a guy who may or may not scream. -He definitely will scream.
jokes.setup: Ross wants to leave, says Tommy may or may not scream
jokes.punchline: Rachel confirms with certainty that Tommy will definitely scream
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag, Rachel's certainty is funny

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Tommy may or may not scream.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, he's definitely going to scream.

jokes.text: Yes!
jokes.setup: Review says Joey is not the worst thing in the production
jokes.punchline: Joey celebrates this backhanded compliment enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Joey's optimism even with bad reviews

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Not the worst thing? That's the best review I've ever gotten!

jokes.text: Thank you, boys and girls. You've ruined my life. Please stuff your talentless faces with my mother's crab cakes!
jokes.setup: Director reads bad reviews of his play
jokes.punchline: Storms out with dramatic insults while still offering snacks
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of dramatic exit with mundane hospitality

dialogue.speaker: Marshall
dialogue.line: You know what? This is ridiculous! You people are philistines! You wouldn't know good theater if it—

dialogue.speaker: Marshall
dialogue.line: But seriously, does anyone want some cookies? I made them fresh this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Marshall
dialogue.line: You're all idiots! Complete and utter idiots! And there's also some brownies in the kitchen if anyone's interested!

jokes.text: Does anyone mind if l save this?
jokes.setup: Director just had dramatic meltdown and stormed out
jokes.punchline: Phoebe wants to save the crab cakes after the outburst
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment, oblivious to drama but focused on food

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Are those crab cakes?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We can't let those go to waste. That's a whole crab that died for nothing.

jokes.text: l've kept myself busy.
jokes.setup: Monica has been on hold for hours
jokes.punchline: Camera reveals she's completely reorganized/redecorated while waiting
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag showing Monica's compulsive cleaning, great character work

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I've been on hold for three hours. THREE HOURS! This is ridiculous!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not just going to sit here and do nothing...

jokes.text: Become a drama critic.
jokes.setup: Kate says critics are failed actors, asks what to do about it
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests the obvious solution of becoming a critic
jokes.explanation: Simple reversal but a bit obvious, fits Joey's straightforward thinking

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: Critics are just failed actors. What do we do about it?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, the obvious solution is to become a critic.

jokes.text: A plague on both your houses!
jokes.setup: Joey is upset about theater criticism
jokes.punchline: Quotes Shakespeare dramatically to curse the critics
jokes.explanation: Joey using Shakespeare is funny given his usual vocabulary level

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? Shakespeare said it best: 'Lord, what fools these mortals be!' And that's what those critics are—fools!

jokes.text: l gave up a job too. -Really? What? -Declawing cats.
jokes.setup: Kate mentions giving up a soap opera role for the play
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals he gave up declawing cats, not another acting job
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up expectation of acting job but delivers something absurd

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: I gave up a soap opera role for this play.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, I gave up declawing cats.

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: That's not the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, it's not. I was actually good at declawing cats.

jokes.text: You're sweet and cute. -l know. That's why they trashed me.
jokes.setup: Kate lists Joey's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Joey takes credit for knowing he's attractive and blames critics for being jealous
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joey's ego and lack of self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: You're smart, you're funny, you're attractive...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. I knew about the attractive part.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The other stuff, I thought people were just being nice.

jokes.text: You never went out with an actress before?
jokes.setup: Joey is confused by Kate's hot and cold behavior
jokes.punchline: Kate implies this erratic behavior is typical for actresses
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about actress stereotypes, somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is going on with you? One minute you're all over me, the next you're treating me like I'm invisible.

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: Welcome to dating an actress, Joey. We're a little... unpredictable.

jokes.text: Why can't l just pick someone like you? -Thanks. -You know what l mean. l mean, like, the sweetest guy.
jokes.setup: Kate wonders why she can't pick someone like Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks it's an insult, Kate clarifies 'sweet' but it still sounds backhanded
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward romantic moment, competent but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: Why can't I pick someone like you?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, is that an insult?

dialogue.speaker: Kate
dialogue.line: No! You're sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That still sounds backhanded.

jokes.text: No, no, l'm fine. And you know why? Because of all the riboflavin.
jokes.setup: Monica has been awake for 24 hours on phone hold
jokes.punchline: Claims she's fine because of vitamins, showing sleep-deprived logic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's stubbornness and sleep deprivation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm fine! I took vitamins.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Vitamins are basically sleep, right?

jokes.text: Yeah, last time l was at Pete's we just watched a movie of us that we'd made earlier.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions staying at Pete's with nothing happening
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they made and watched their own sex tape
jokes.explanation: Typical sitcom oversharing moment, gets reaction but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We stayed at Pete's with nothing happening.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, we made our own sex tape and watched it.

jokes.text: l'm so, so sorry. l am definitely gonna see your play. l swear, your play is very important to us. Thank you for your patience. Your play is the next play l'm gonna see.
jokes.setup: Monica apologizes to Joey for missing his play
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses phone hold message language when talking to Joey
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing how phone hold has affected Monica's speech patterns

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, I'm so sorry I missed your play. I feel terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's okay, Mon.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, really. Your call is very important to us. Please hold while we try to locate an usher.

jokes.text: You had 'the night'! When two people realize their feelings for each other and they talk for hours and learn all about the other person. -Did you learn about her family? -Two brothers. One died! -Yes!
jokes.setup: Monica explains romantic concept of 'the night' and asks for proof
jokes.punchline: Joey's excited revelation about dead brother gets enthusiastic approval
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect escalation - celebrating learning about death as romantic progress

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know, 'the night' is supposed to be romantic. Something special between two people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I don't think that's what Joey means by 'the night.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! I got one! My brother died!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no - that's my 'the night' story! Isn't that great?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's... that's not romantic, Joey.

jokes.text: Sorry. She thought you were somebody else.
jokes.setup: Waiter brings Ross a drink from woman at bar
jokes.punchline: Returns immediately to say it was mistaken identity
jokes.explanation: Simple rejection joke, gets a laugh but nothing special

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: This drink is from the woman at the bar.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Really? Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Actually, she just asked me to tell you it was a mistake. She thought you were someone else.

jokes.text: 7:1 5. -Watch doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Monica asks what time it is, Phoebe gives specific time
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals her watch doesn't actually work
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdity delivered matter-of-factly, good economy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What time is it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How do you know that?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have a watch.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Does it work?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Yeah, and at the end of the play he got up, and he just started, like, banging his hands together.
jokes.setup: Ross is listing Tommy's aggressive behaviors
jokes.punchline: Describes applauding as aggressive 'banging his hands together'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - makes normal applause sound violent, perfect Ross overthinking

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He was banging his hands together.

jokes.text: What is in the cup? -Okay, it's coffee. -lced coffee? Tell me it's iced coffee. -lt's hot-- -Hot coffee! You idiot! You gonna spill hot coffee all over me, huh? What are you? Just a big, stupid doofy idiot with a doofy idiot hairdo?
jokes.setup: Tommy asks about Rachel's drink
jokes.punchline: Explodes into rage about hot coffee and insults Rachel's appearance
jokes.explanation: Payoff of Tommy's character arc, absurd escalation over harmless coffee

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: What are you drinking?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's hot coffee.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: Hot coffee?! HOT COFFEE?! Do you have any idea what you're doing to yourself?! This is ridiculous! And another thing — your hair looks terrible!

jokes.text: Sorry! Sorry l'm late! My duck and my chick had a fight. lt was ugly.
jokes.setup: Joey arrives late to his own play
jokes.punchline: Explains delay was due to his pets having a fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - treats pet fight as serious emergency, great character consistency

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry I'm late! My dog and my parrot got into this huge fight at home.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I had to break it up, and then I had to take them both to the vet to make sure they were okay.

jokes.text: Gross! ldiot! Stupid, little, fuzzy, yellow creature! Oh, look at me! l'm so cute! l'm a little chick who's disgusting! You're so stupid! How are you not yet extinct? What are you quacking about? Dumb Donald Dodo!
jokes.setup: Tommy encounters Joey's chick
jokes.punchline: Has complete meltdown screaming at innocent baby animal with insults
jokes.explanation: Peak Tommy moment - screaming at baby chick with full theatrical insults, absurd escalation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you wanna hold the chick?

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: Oh, sure!

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: What is this?! What are you doing here?! You think you're so cute with your little fuzzy body and your tiny little feet! Well, you're not! You're a bad chick! A bad, bad chick!

jokes.text: l'm gonna get on this spaceship and l'm gonna go to Blaargon-7 in search of alternative fuels. But when l return 200 years from now you'll be long gone but l won't have aged at all.
jokes.setup: Joey's character is saying goodbye to his love interest
jokes.punchline: Reveals the play involves time travel and alien planets
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd reveal of sci-fi elements in what seemed like realistic play

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I gotta tell you something. This whole time we've been together, it's meant everything to me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But I just found out my character in the play? He travels through time and gets abducted by aliens on planet Zorgon-5.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So... I guess this is goodbye. For now. And also for 200 years in the future. And also in an alternate dimension.

jokes.text: Phoebe, 800 is toll free. 801 is Utah.
jokes.setup: Phoebe thinks she's been calling toll-free number for two days
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals she's been calling Utah the whole time
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to phone subplot - Monica wasn't even calling the right number

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, so you're telling me I've been calling a toll-free number for two days?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Not exactly. You've been calling Utah the whole time.

jokes.text: Yeah, every big Utah-based company has one.
jokes.setup: Phoebe insists big companies have 800 numbers
jokes.punchline: Chandler plays along with deadpan about Utah companies
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the Utah reveal, Chandler's sarcastic agreement works well

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: All the big companies have 800 numbers.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're right. But you know what? The really big companies? They have 900 numbers.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah. And if you call them, they make money off of you instead of the other way around.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: In fact, the BIGGEST companies, like in Utah, they don't have any numbers at all. You just have to know where they are.

jokes.text: Well, l think l broke it. But that's all right. Here's the number where you can call.
jokes.setup: Monica finally hangs up the phone after days
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers to give them the phone number to call back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - even after hanging up, still wants to help/control the situation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Immediately offers to give them the phone number to call back

tmdb_overview: When Rachel finds out that Ross is bringing a date to the premiere of Joey's big play, she finds one of her own. Ross alone concludes that her date is insane. Poor Phoebe is on hold for days with a company she needs to talk to before her phone warranty expires. Meanwhile, Joey's play gets terrible reviews, but his co-star provides some comfort.
insight: Escalating character comedy drives middling season finale despite solid joke density of 2.38 per minute.
